We met with two teams in the morning. They asked many insightful questions… I explained in detail how BitMEX operated, including malicious trading products and the shared loss margin system. A few weeks later, I learned that the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) was not interested in our license application. The matter stalled until more than two years later, when the U.S. Department of Justice and the CFTC filed criminal and civil lawsuits against us for violating the Bank Secrecy Act and the Commodity Exchange Act. I was incredibly naive to think the CFTC would appreciate my reasonable arguments. Like all regulatory agencies, the CFTC's role is to maintain the status quo. If they believe the current market structure is perfect enough, no amount of logic can convince them to allow competition. Global regulators are reluctant to allow genuine financial innovation because it could lead to the downfall of their domestic financial giants. It's worth mentioning that SBF exploited the mistakes we made at BitMEX—though he is not commendable. He and his politically connected parents were trying to achieve the same goal: bringing criminals to the U.S. to confront the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) head-on, and his method was: the American way—donating huge sums of money to politicians (primarily Democrats). When you ask people why they invested in FTX, or why they believed SBF would succeed, they mention that SBF's parents were professors at Stanford University and had close ties to the Democratic Party. I don't know the current situation, but they were one of the largest campaign fundraisers for the Democratic Party at the time. When we talk about campaign finance in the United States, the terminology we use carries a certain legitimacy. We talk about "donations" and "fundraising"; but when the West talks about other countries and their campaign finance systems, they talk about "bribery" and "corruption." Western investors blinded by SBF are euphemistically saying that SBF legally bribed "the right people." Some critics might argue that I'm just a sore loser, taking advantage of the other party's predicament to criticize them. But don't forget that by mid-2022, FTX was almost certain to obtain a banking license to issue its own stablecoin, as well as DCM and DCO licenses from the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Looking at the image gallery below, you might think SBF is a reliable partner to those in power, even as his company attempts to destroy one of their largest corporate donors—the banking system. The stereotype is that a "white boy" walks into a lion's den and gets a hug; this "black boy" walks in and gets condemned. That's the reality; to understand more deeply how SBF uses stereotypes to his own advantage at the expense of his clients, investors, and global regulators, read my article, "White Boy". SBF and FTX.us donated nearly $100 million to his campaign, and if he weren't such a hopeless case of risk management and trading, FTX would be larger than Binance today. Wow, so cute. U.S. Representative Maxine Waters (SBF, right) served as chair of the powerful House Financial Services Committee from 2020 to 2022, during FTX's heyday. See, it has nothing to do with race. This is precisely the post-racist American future envisioned by Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. in his 1963 "I Have a Dream" speech.

Former US President Biden appointed Caroline Pham as a commissioner of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) in early 2022. The SBF certainly knows how to cultivate relationships with key figures. My closest encounter with a CFTC commissioner was at a cocktail party hosted by the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) in Boca Raton, Florida in 2018.

Money makes the world go round…

The FTX/Alameda incident dealt a heavy blow to the tolerance of US and overseas regulatory agencies and traditional finance (TradFi) towards cryptocurrencies. This is because the US Democratic Party's attitude towards cryptocurrencies shifted from ambivalence to outright hostility. Some political figures trusted SBF because of his prestigious family background; his parents were both heavyweight political figures, fitting the standard of a "white elite." To clear themselves of suspicion of collusion with SBF's scam, they began to try to prove that they had always opposed this fraudulent cryptocurrency scheme.

Therefore, from late 2022 to early 2025, US regulatory agencies launched a fierce crackdown on US cryptocurrency practitioners until President Trump was re-elected. The transformation of Trump and his family from cryptocurrency opponents to supporters deserves in-depth exploration. Trump's support for cryptocurrency was both a shrewd political strategy and a form of retaliation. It was a shrewd political strategy because the Democratic Party alienated a wealthy, active, and growing group. The transformative nature of cryptocurrency allowed SBF and other companies to amass billions of dollars in wealth in just a few years. America's cryptocurrency upstarts eagerly endorsed any political figure they deemed worthy of their support. Trump and his "Red Republican team" raised hundreds of millions of dollars from wealthy individual cryptocurrency donors, corporations, and political action committees (PACs). In 2024, this funding helped Trump overwhelmingly defeat Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris and helped the Republicans regain control of the House and Senate. This was one of the most glorious victories in modern American political history. This is retaliation because Trump's opponents broke the rules of the game by targeting his family's bank accounts after he left office in 2020. Trump is part of the establishment. Just look at his photos with celebrities in New York—the imperial capital—over the past 40 years. Trump's opponents crossed the line by launching criminal and economic attacks against him. Usually, when the elite clashed during the quadrennial presidential election fiasco, it was merely to show the public that Democrats and Republicans had different political platforms, when in reality, their common goal was to maintain the long-term stability of the elite. No matter what the previous president did, the next administration would always give him a break. Even Nixon was pardoned. But not Trump. After losing the 2020 election, he was convicted on various civil and criminal charges. To further damage Trump's defeat, Democrats closed the Trump family's bank accounts from 2021 to 2025. Trump initially dismissed cryptocurrencies with mere indifference, but now he and his family have finally grasped the dangers of banks. They have also recognized the necessity of a non-political hard currency like Bitcoin. Trump's embrace of Bitcoin and cryptocurrencies stems from desperation. If you listen to his sons, they explicitly state that one of the main reasons their family is fully committed to cryptocurrencies is their terrible experience with the banking system during Biden's presidency. Now that Trump is back, it's time to dismantle the financial system that nearly destroyed his family and business empire, because it is this system that allows cryptocurrencies to compete with traditional finance. Therefore, by 2025, traditional finance will either adapt to change or be rendered obsolete by cryptocurrencies and other innovative technologies. I review the history of cryptocurrency regulation in the United States for two reasons. First, most global traditional financial regulators blindly follow the United States like lemmings. This is a self-protective mechanism. No regulatory agency will lose its job for following U.S. financial regulations. But if an agency acts differently and suffers adverse consequences, it will certainly be ousted. Therefore, if the U.S. accepts cryptocurrencies for some reason, it will allow other regulatory agencies to do the same. For example, the Singapore Exchange (SGX) launched its own cryptocurrency immediately after Trump re-allowed traditional financial regulators to accept cryptocurrencies. Secondly, I want to use this history to demonstrate that this situation will not change, at least not until Trump's term ends in 2029. By then, the largest derivative of the S&P 500 or Nasdaq 100 will be perpetual contracts traded on cryptocurrency exchanges, not contracts traded on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME). By then, even if the next "peaceful emperor under American rule" opposes cryptocurrencies, their influence will be too great to be shaken. Before delving into how perpetual contracts will completely disrupt stock trading, I want to discuss their influence from the perspective of the wealth accumulation of a few typical perpetual contract holders. I believe Binance co-founder Chengpeng Zhao is one of the top ten richest non-political leaders in the world. He amassed such a massive fortune in less than a decade. I think CZ's wealth growth stems directly from Binance's rise to become the world's largest perpetual contract exchange after Bitcoin prices plummeted due to the COVID-19 pandemic in March 2020, surpassing BitMEX. Binance elevated the popularity of perpetual contracts to a new level, and coupled with its dominance in altcoin trading, it created a cryptocurrency exchange giant. SBF created FTX after trading perpetual contracts for his hedge fund Alameda on BitMEX. Thanks to FTX's rapid growth (FTX essentially only offers perpetual contracts), SBF became a billionaire at the fastest pace in history (at least on paper). **Hyperliquid founder Jeff Yan** may already be a billionaire, or at least very close. He created the fastest-growing decentralized exchange (DEX), and if my stablecoin theory comes true, he could eventually become the largest exchange in human history. The next wave of billionaires in the cryptocurrency exchange space will come from the intersection of decentralized exchanges and stablecoin exchanges. **Stock Perpetual Contracts** Traditional financial groups (TradFi) are striving to maintain their dominance in stock trading. The public stock market is politically and economically crucial for vested interests. It will be very interesting to see how they respond to the rapid rise of stock perpetual contracts. These perpetual contracts will first dominate the offshore trading of US stock price risk. U.S. stocks, and indeed all stocks, will eventually be tokenized. But perpetual stock contracts don't need tokenization to succeed. Perpetual stock contracts already have fertile ground to grow. The U.S. stock market is the world's largest. Large U.S. tech companies like Nvidia have market capitalizations exceeding the annual GDP of most countries. Almost everyone globally uses their products. Yet, most retail investors cannot trade these stocks. These retail investors are accustomed to trading cryptocurrencies with high leverage anytime, anywhere. If Jaewon in Seoul could sell his Nvidia stock on the subway home from work, during his dead-end chaebol job, just like trading Bitcoin, he would certainly embrace it. This is the potential of perpetual stock contracts. Currently, daily trading volume for perpetual stock contracts exceeds $100 million. As traders and market makers become more familiar with the contract details, daily trading volume will soon reach billions of dollars. With a surge of breaking political, military, and economic news following the close of traditional financial markets on Friday nights, perpetual stock contracts will become a primary means for institutional and retail traders to hedge risk over the weekend. This will force major U.S. stock exchanges to implement 24/7 trading more quickly. Will it be cryptocurrency speculators or suited bankers who ultimately win the perpetual stock contract market? That depends on whether traditional financial regulators allow clearinghouses to offer socialized loss mechanisms. I predict that by the end of 2026, price discovery for the largest U.S. tech stocks and major stock indices (such as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100) will primarily occur in the retail-oriented perpetual contract market. I would be pleased if, in time, financial media outlets used the ticker symbols of S&P 500 component perpetual contracts as the best source of pricing information, rather than the Globex version from the CME Group. It's not too late; the CME and other exchanges have all the advantages, along with a large pool of smart and proactive employees. Perhaps they will see this article and reflect on it seriously. They have no reason to allow a group of cryptocurrency speculators to bypass intermediaries, especially when regulators are vying to champion exchanges. The Last Frontier The world's most traded derivatives contract is the CME Group's SOFR futures contract. Trading volume in the fixed-income market far exceeds that of the stock, forex, and cryptocurrency markets. My challenge to the cryptocurrency community is to create a completely new derivative that allows retail investors to speculate on interest rates in entirely new ways. The Pendle team is working on this, and their Boros protocol is rapidly gaining market acceptance. But this space remains full of opportunities, and many innovators can showcase their ideas here.

data.title: The x402 payment ecosystem is ushering in its own "Taobao moment".
data.description:
Author: Yue Xiaoyu; Source: X, @yuexiaoyu111

x402 payment ecosystem is ushering in its own "Taobao moment"!

1. What is the most important thing in building a payment ecosystem?

It is actually the merchant ecosystem, that is, you need to enrich the supply side first in order to attract more demand.

Analogous to the emergence of Alipay, the Taobao platform existed first, and Alipay was built to solve the payment problem.

In other words, electronic payment was rapidly popularized because of the emergence and prosperity of e-commerce platforms.

The popularity of Alipay is not because of its superior technology, but because Taobao's merchant ecosystem (supply) drove demand.

2. AI-native payment also faces the challenge of a cold start

The previously popular x402 is a very simple and pure AI-native payment protocol, mainly solving the problem of AI payment.

In developing its ecosystem, x402 faces the same challenges as when electronic payments first emerged: Where is the market? Who are the merchants? Who will supply the data? Now, the largest seller has appeared in the x402 payment ecosystem: the decentralized data provider Switchboard. Switchboard is an oracle and is currently the first and only decentralized data source in the x402 ecosystem that supports pay-per-use access. Simply put, x402 allows AI Agents to easily buy data, and Switchboard is the seller of high-quality, real-time data, using x402 to collect micropayments. 3. Switchboard is the "Taobao moment" of the x402 payment ecosystem! Just as Taobao first gathered a massive number of merchants, Switchboard first provided a rich and reliable supply of paid data, attracting the first batch of AI Agents to test it. Switchboard uses x402 to list all data sources for sale: AI Agent sends a request → Server returns a 402 error → Agent automatically makes on-chain payments → Data is obtained instantly. This forms a flywheel: Switchboard's supply attracts AI Agents (demand) → Transaction volume increases → More sellers follow x402 → Ecosystem prosperity. 4. In a payment ecosystem, supply must come first! Building a payment ecosystem requires first creating a marketplace like Taobao, enriching merchants and activating supply. x402 + Switchboard perfectly replicates Alipay + Taobao: the protocol provides the tools, and the supply side (data sellers) provides the market. a16z estimates the x402 market to reach $30T, and Switchboard, as a pioneering data marketplace, has the opportunity to capture a huge share of the data layer. We can continue to follow the latest developments in the x402 ecosystem.

data.title: Why are privacy coins rising against the trend when the crypto market is weakening?
data.description:
Author: Bradley Peak, Source: Cointelegraph, Translated by: Shaw Jinse Finance

Key Takeaways

  • Prices of privacy coins like Zcash rose, while the total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies and the price of Bitcoin saw a significant decline.

  • This rebound occurred against a backdrop of tightening policies, including pressure from the Financial Action Task Force (FATF), new EU anti-money laundering rules, and the increasing number of privacy coins being delisted.

  • Sanctions and prosecutions involving mixers and wallets have raised questions about the boundaries between infrastructure and money transfers, prompting compliance teams to take cautious de-risking measures.

  • Analysts are divided on this, with some seeing it as a protest against surveillance, while others see it as a surge in vulnerability at the end of the economic cycle amid shrinking high-risk sectors. Over the past six weeks, the cryptocurrency market capitalization has evaporated by more than $1 trillion, as traders have dumped speculative assets. Total market capitalization has fallen from a peak of over $4.3 trillion in early October to just over $3.1 trillion, a drop of approximately 25%-28%. Bitcoin's price has fallen nearly 30% from its all-time high of over $126,000 in early October and is currently trading just over $90,000. Against this backdrop, one of the strongest performing sectors, and also the most volatile, is privacy coins. Since late summer, Zcash's price has surged a hundredfold, with its market capitalization soaring from less than $1 billion in August to over $7 billion in early November. It briefly surpassed Monero to become the most valuable privacy coin. Meanwhile, Zcash rapidly climbed to the top of Coinbase's internal search rankings, with user search volume exceeding that of Bitcoin and XRP, indicating a rise in retail investor interest. Analysts say the combined effect of soaring prices and surging search volume foreshadows a typical hot trade. The complexity lies in the fact that this is happening in a market facing increasing regulatory pressure, exchange delistings, and sanctions-related scrutiny. Privacy Coins as Outliers: Data and Narratives The latest surge was clearly led by Zcash (ZEC), with Monero (XMR) lagging far behind. Key data cited by analysts includes: On some major trading platforms, ZEC has risen by over 200% in a month. From its late summer lows, ZEC's peer-to-peer volatility has achieved triple-digit percentage gains. XMR has also risen, but by a much smaller margin than ZEC, which briefly allowed ZEC's market capitalization to surpass XMR's. Despite the rebound, ZEC is still trading well below its all-time high. Explanations can be broadly categorized into two types: One focuses on structural and technical factors, including the reduced issuance as the halving progresses and the planned NU6.1 upgrade, which will transfer more control of funds to token holders. The other points to narrative and market structure, including overly high investor expectations for prices, regulatory concerns, a thin order book, and short squeezes in relatively small market segments. Most observers believe this rally comes at a time when regulatory and policy shifts are unfavorable to anonymous assets.

    Regulation is moving in the opposite direction

    Globally, privacy coins are entirely within the scope of anti-money laundering (AML) discussions.

    Since 2019, the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) has applied all of its AML and counter-terrorist financing (CFT) standards to virtual assets and virtual asset service providers (VASPs), including the "travel rule" requiring the inclusion of sender and recipient information in eligible transfers.

    A targeted update in 2024 found that approximately three-quarters of the jurisdictions assessed were still only partially or completely non-compliant with Article 15 recommendations, and approximately 30% had not yet legally implemented the travel rule. The FATF also specifically noted the concerning trend of illicit actors increasingly using cryptocurrencies that enhance anonymity.

    In Europe, the direction is clearer.

    According to legal and policy analysis, new EU-wide anti-money laundering rules, centered on Regulation 2024/1624 and related legislation, will ban the use of anonymous cryptocurrency accounts and privacy coins on licensed platforms by 2027. Crypto asset service providers will be required to implement bank-style anti-money laundering controls, verify the beneficial owners behind wallets interacting with their services, and gradually phase out support for fully anonymous tools. However, this does not mean that holding these assets is illegal everywhere. But it does mean that infrastructure is being redesigned in most regulated financial systems, predicated on privacy tokens being restricted or excluded. Delisting, shrinking trading venues, and liquidity risks: The regulatory environment has begun to reshape where and how privacy coins are traded. Key Shifts: In 2024, nearly 60 privacy coins were delisted from centralized exchanges, the highest number since 2021. Monero saw the most removals, with Dash (DASH) and several other cryptocurrencies also affected as exchanges re-evaluated their anti-money laundering policies. Binance restricted or canceled trading of XMR, ZEC, and DASH for users in several European jurisdictions, citing local regulations and compliance. Kraken announced at the end of 2024 that it would cease XMR trading and deposit services for European Economic Area (EEA) customers, with withdrawals ending at the end of the year, explicitly mentioning EU regulatory changes, including the Crypto Asset Markets (MiCA) framework. These measures could create typical liquidity problems. During market rallies, thinly traded markets can experience significant volatility due to relatively small inflows. As trading shifts from well-funded large platforms to smaller or less regulated ones, it becomes more difficult for large holders to exit the market without causing price volatility. The same structure can trigger sudden spikes or increase the risk of price "vacuums" during market downturns. Sanctions Spillover Effects, Court Proceedings, and Compliance Anxiety Sanctions and enforcement actions add another layer of uncertainty. In 2022, the U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) imposed sanctions on Tornado Cash, accusing the Ethereum-based mixer of laundering billions of dollars. In late 2024, a U.S. appeals court ruled that sanctioning immutable smart contracts exceeded the Treasury Department's authority. In March 2025, OFAC officially lifted sanctions against Tornado Cash. However, the legal risks did not disappear. Tornado Cash's developers faced criminal prosecutions in multiple jurisdictions, with one co-founder convicted of operating an unlicensed money transfer business. Another case involving Samourai Wallet sent a similar signal. In November 2025, its founder pleaded guilty in the United States and was sentenced to several years in prison for conspiring to operate an unlicensed money transfer company. Prosecutors alleged that over $2 billion in Bitcoin was transferred through the service. For compliance teams, the line between infrastructure and money transfer tools is difficult to draw. Some anti-money laundering service providers and policy agencies now classify privacy coins, mixers, and some high-risk decentralized finance (DeFi) tools in the same high-risk category. Under pressure from the FATF and national regulators, many companies tend to over-compliance, such as freezing deposits related to privacy tools, refusing listings, and restricting payment uses. For users, this presents secondary risks. Even if a particular cryptocurrency or protocol is not sanctioned, its surrounding ecosystem may still deem it too risky to engage with. Analysts are divided on what this surge signifies: Some see it as a protest trade against increasing on-chain surveillance, data-sharing rules, and sanctions screening. Others believe it's a speculative surge in the late stages of a shrinking segment, driven more by leverage and narrative than by long-term demand. Key policy milestones: EU anti-money laundering regulations restrict or effectively prohibit the issuance of privacy coins on authorized platforms, expected to be fully implemented around 2027. The FATF will continue to publish implementation review reports; the latest report states that compliance with virtual asset standards and the "travel rule" remains incomplete in most jurisdictions. On the technology front, upgrades like Zcash's NU6.1 fund transfer feature and experiments with optional privacy layers on major networks may test whether stronger privacy protections can coexist with regulators' requirements for traceability. Currently, privacy coins are caught between the long-standing debate on financial privacy and increasingly stringent global anti-money laundering and sanctions mechanisms. Understanding legal, liquidity, and enforcement risks is crucial to understanding how this field operates.

data.title: Hong Kong’s Crypto Industry Donates Over HK$40 Million to Support Tai Po Fire Emergency Efforts: Here’s How You Can Help
data.description:
Tai Po Blaze Sparks Rapid Crypto-Led Relief In Hong Kong

Flames engulfed Wang Fuk Court in Tai Po since 26 November, sweeping through eight high-rise residential blocks and affecting more than 1,900 flats.

At least four people are dead and three others are injured after a major fire engulfed a residential apartment complex in Hong Kong's Tai Po district on Wednesday, with photos and video from the scene appearing to show serious damage to several buildings. https://t.co/bXXM22GCfQpic.twitter.com/NmGhkKHcY0

— ABC News (@ABC) November 26, 2025

The blaze, classified as a No. 5 alarm which is the highest level in Hong Kong, has claimed at least 94 lives, including a firefighter, and injured 76, among them 11 firefighters.

Full-scale rescue operations are still on-going.

Authorities have traced the rapid spread of the fire to highly flammable styrofoam used in renovation works that have been underway since July 2024, shrouded in bamboo scaffolding and green mesh.

Three individuals connected to the contractor behind the renovations, two directors and a consultant, have been arrested for manslaughter, while separate graft investigations are underway regarding the HK$330 million repair project.

How Crypto Companies Moved Faster Than Traditional Institutions

As emergency services battled the blaze, Hong Kong’s crypto industry mounted a swift and visible relief effort.

Within hours, major exchanges announced donations running into millions of Hong Kong dollars to support rescue operations and aid affected residents.

Binance led the charge with HK$10 million, matched by local heavyweight HashKey.

Gate.io contributed HK$10 million, while KuCoin added HK$2 million, as confirmed through PAnews and Foresight News coverage.

與香港同在

Gate 已緊急設立 1,000 萬港幣援助基金，用於支援香港大埔火災受災居民的生活救助與社區重建。我們將根據實際需求分階段撥付、持續投入，讓援助抵達每一位受影響的居民…

— Gate 华语 (@Gate_zh) November 27, 2025

With HKD 2 million in support, KuCoin stands together with Hong Kong as the community recovers from the Tai Po incident.

Strength and care go hand in hand. 🖤

Details: https://t.co/gDQIO5cvdA#TogetherWeStand#HongKongpic.twitter.com/ASqqM5XhGJ

— KuCoin (@kucoincom) November 27, 2025

OKX has also stepped up, contributing HK$10 million to support urgent relief efforts and help Hong Kong recover.

Today, we stand with Hong Kong 🇭🇰

In moments of crisis, courage shows itself and Hong Kong is showing the world what strength looks like.

We’re contributing HK$10 million to support urgent relief efforts and help the city recover. We’re here and we’re committed. pic.twitter.com/XSkwKmPAXj

— OKX (@okx) November 27, 2025

Animoca Brands went further, opening dedicated crypto wallets to channel donations directly to the Hong Kong Red Cross and local charities.

The company invited global contributors to donate via EVM-compatible and Solana chains, pledging to convert all tokens into Hong Kong dollars and cover conversion fees, with the proceeds fully directed to relief efforts.

Yat Siu, co-founder and executive chairman of Animoca Brands, said,

“As a Hong Kong headquartered company, we are heartbroken by the tragedy and horror of the fire in Tai Po.

We encourage the global crypto community to help fund relief and recovery efforts for the thousands of people affected by this disaster, either by donating directly to relief organizations or via the wallets we have set up to facilitate crypto donations of liquid tokens.”

As of 28 November, the total pledged by the crypto sector exceeds HK$40 million (~US$5.14 million).

币圈的捐款和传统企业虽然有些差距，但是大差不差了：
币安，okex, gate，hsk等都捐款了1000万港币，
kucoin 300万，另外还有100万的，还有孙哥的口头承诺。 pic.twitter.com/ruH9mWER7X

— 2140 (@2140io) November 28, 2025

Justin Sun has also expressed support and an intention to donate, though no confirmed transaction or amount has yet been reported.

I am deeply saddened to learn about the recent fire in Tai Po, Hong Kong. My thoughts are with all the residents and families affected by this tragic incident.

To support the relief efforts, I will be making a donation to assist those who have suffered losses and to help with…

— H.E. Justin Sun 👨‍🚀 🌞 (@justinsuntron) November 27, 2025

Crypto Response Shows Commitment to Community

HashKey and Binance issued relief statements within 24 hours, offering both financial support and logistical assistance while the fire remained a top news story.

💛心繫香港：幣安將向香港捐款1000萬港幣支持救援重建

為切實協助救災及後續重建工作，幣安將通過相關渠道向災區進行捐贈，支持宏福苑居民重建家園，略盡綿薄之力。

我們向所有大埔受災民眾致以深切慰問，祈願逝者安息，生者堅強🙏 pic.twitter.com/A4rThj5kgZ

— 币安Binance华语 (@binancezh) November 27, 2025

Our hearts are with all those affected by the tragic Tai Po fire in Hong Kong, a city that is home to the core of the HashKey community.

We are committed to supporting our community and are donating HK$10 million to aid emergency relief efforts and assist impacted residents.… pic.twitter.com/hLLCnjiqne

— HashKey Group (@HashKeyGroup) November 27, 2025

This rapid engagement contrasts with slower responses from traditional financial institutions and highlights the growing influence and integration of crypto firms in Hong Kong’s social and economic fabric.

Rebuilding Trust And Credibility Through Community Action

Hong Kong is actively positioning itself as Asia’s digital asset hub, drawing exchanges, venture firms, and token issuers under new licensing frameworks.

In this context, supporting local communities during a disaster reinforces public trust and strengthens the industry’s standing with regulators, who are increasingly scrutinising corporate responsibility.

The Human Cost Remains Severe

Despite the swift response, the human toll is stark.

At least 94 people have died, 76 are injured, and dozens remain unaccounted for amid the smouldering ruins.

Firefighters continue to carry out forcible searches to ensure no residents remain trapped.

Hong Kong authorities said on Friday the death toll from the city's worst fire in decades had risen to at least 94, with the blaze almost entirely extinguished and rescuers scouring torched high-rise buildings for scores of people still listed as missing. https://t.co/1RwXrMUnFWpic.twitter.com/AcMHggvd85

— AFP News Agency (@AFP) November 27, 2025

Early reports listed 279 missing, though figures have not been updated; Deputy Fire Services Director Derek Chan said that 25 calls for help remain unresolved, with three new cases prioritised.

The Tai Po tragedy is one of the deadliest residential fires Hong Kong has faced in nearly 80 years, and the ongoing rescue, criminal, and corruption investigations underscore the scale of the disaster.

In the midst of this, the speed and scale of the crypto sector’s response have drawn attention, offering relief to the community while demonstrating a commitment beyond business interests.

How You Can Help Through Crypto Donations

data.title: XRP ETFs Ignite Institutional Frenzy as Bitcoin Reclaims $91K in Year-End Surge
data.description:
Bitcoin Reclaims $91K as XRP ETFs Rewrite the Institutional Playbook

Bitcoin’s (BTC) rebound toward $91,000 marks a decisive recovery from its recent dip to the $82,000 zone, with macro tailwinds — chiefly expectations of an imminent Federal Reserve rate cut — re-energizing market sentiment.

But in an unusual turn for a market normally dominated by Bitcoin-driven narratives, XRP ETFs have stolen the spotlight, accelerating past expectations and reshaping institutional allocation trends heading into the final month of 2025.

JUST IN: ETF clients buy $21.81 million worth of $XRP.

Total XRP assets held by ETFs - $676.49 million. pic.twitter.com/z9aGO7Y2Tg

— Whale Insider (@WhaleInsider) November 27, 2025

XRP investment products have seen $676 million in assets under management, buoyed by weekly inflows topping $230 million and daily volumes above $38 million.

Major players such as Bitwise, Canary Capital, and Franklin Templeton have contributed to the surge — not chasing momentum, but executing what analysts describe as a “durability-first strategy.” This means that institutions are now less concerned with short-term volatility and more focused on building exposure to regulated, long-horizon products calibrated for depth rather than speed.

XRP End-of-Day Recap — November 27, 2025

Quiet Moves, Loud Implications

XRP ETFs just inhaled $21.81M (Nov 27 est., via SoSoValue) — faster than a black hole. Bitwise led the surge, and institutional demand hasn’t eased for a second.

Mini Snapshot:
Cumulative XRP Spot ETF… pic.twitter.com/fUA63pdPkw

— CryptoDownUnder (@adoniscoxy) November 28, 2025

In contrast, the Bitcoin ETFs are getting less tailwind despite its price strength. Analysts suggest that this could be due to how the Bitcoin's ETF market tends to be more mature and deeply priced in, whereas XRP — newly legitimized by ETF availability — is experiencing its “initial institutional discovery phase.” That asymmetry helps explain why XRP ETFs are attracting heavy inflows at the same moment Bitcoin ETFs show tepid behavior.

Bitcoin, meanwhile, has stabilized above the psychological $90,000 threshold, rekindling mid-to-long-term bullish scenarios even as U.S. holiday conditions suppress spot market volumes. Ethereum, Binance Coin, and Solana have also tracked upward, while ADA lags behind after a period of underperformance.

Whale Movements, Derivatives Signals, and the Return of Risk Appetite

Beneath the surface of the price recovery lies a complex redistribution of liquidity. On-chain data shows that 700,000 BTC moved between long-term holders within 48 hours, while 9,000 BTC flowed onto exchanges in a single day. Rising average deposits on Binance — climbing from 12 BTC to 37 BTC per address — suggest a phase of strategic profit-taking and portfolio lightening, not capitulation. The market is repositioning rather than exiting.

Smart money is waking up.

In the last couple of weeks, 91 new whale wallets (100+ $BTC) appeared on-chain, while tiny wallets (0.1 BTC and below) keep vanishing.

Retail is panic-selling the bottom.
Whales are buying the fear.

Retail exhaustion + whale accumulation has always… pic.twitter.com/pBIhIlI8RD

— Niels (@Web3Niels) November 26, 2025

In derivatives markets, sentiment is beginning to flip. Traders have largely abandoned protective put concentrations around $80,000–$85,000, shifting instead toward call targets at $100,000. Funding rates have turned positive again, signaling a renewed appetite for leveraged long exposure — a subtle but meaningful change after weeks of caution. Options desks describe it as “a breath returning to the market.”

ETF flows reinforce this reawakening. While Bitcoin products continue to see rotation rather than strong net inflows, multi-asset crypto ETFs and altcoin-specific products — including the newly approved Bitwise Dogecoin ETF — demonstrate that institutional allocators are widening their scope rather than retreating. This raises the question: is this a measured return to risk, or an early sign of broader speculative engagement?

Introducing the Bitwise Dogecoin ETF. $BWOW starts trading tomorrow.

We weren’t expecting to launch this product, but the $DOGE community requested, nay demanded, this.

Dogecoin is:
- Seventh-largest crypto asset by market weight (as of 11/25/25)
- $22B market cap
- $1B traded… pic.twitter.com/szf1s72kdl

— Bitwise (@BitwiseInvest) November 25, 2025

Key markers of the current landscape include Bitcoin trading near $91,458, XRP ETFs holding $676.49M, a spike in whale-level repositioning, and a record 3.8 long/short ratio among Binance’s largest accounts. Together, these signals point toward a year-end environment defined not by euphoria, but by strategic reinvestment driven by institutional structures, regulatory clarity, and shifting macro expectations.

As 2025 nears its close, the crypto market appears to be transitioning from defensive posturing to cautious optimism — with XRP ETFs unexpectedly leading the charge and Bitcoin regaining altitude as the ecosystem prepares for its next macro-driven chapter.
